text.title: LlamaIndex: Query Engines and Retrieval
text.summary: Query engine configuration, response modes, hybrid retrieval, reranking, and routing patterns for LlamaIndex.

rule.statement: Set similarity_top_k=5-10 and response_mode="refine" for complex questions; use response_mode="compact" for speed-sensitive endpoints. Always use async (aquery, aretrieve) in production.
rule.rationale: compact uses a single LLM call (fast, lower quality); refine iterates over retrieved chunks (slower, higher quality for multi-chunk synthesis). Sync calls block event loops when deployed with FastAPI or async frameworks.

example.code:
from llama_index.core.query_engine import RetrieverQueryEngine from llama_index.core.postprocessor import SentenceTransformerRerank from llama_index.core import get_response_synthesizer
reranker = SentenceTransformerRerank( model="cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-2-v2", top_n=5, ) query_engine = index.as_query_engine( similarity_top_k=20, node_postprocessors=[reranker], response_mode="compact", ) # In async context: response = await query_engine.aquery("What is the refund policy?")
example.note: Retrieve top-20, rerank to top-5. Reranking top-5 after reranking beats top-20 without it.

rule.statement: Combine BM25 and vector search via QueryFusionRetriever with reciprocal_rerank mode as the default retrieval strategy; weight vector at 0.6 and BM25 at 0.4.
rule.rationale: Pure vector search misses exact-match queries (product codes, names, technical terms). BM25 covers lexical precision; vector covers semantic generalization. Reciprocal rank fusion is stable across query types.

example.code:
from llama_index.retrievers.bm25 import BM25Retriever from llama_index.core.retrievers import QueryFusionRetriever
bm25_retriever = BM25Retriever.from_defaults(nodes=nodes, similarity_top_k=10) vector_retriever = index.as_retriever(similarity_top_k=10)
hybrid_retriever = QueryFusionRetriever( retrievers=[bm25_retriever, vector_retriever], retriever_weights=[0.4, 0.6], num_queries=1, mode="reciprocal_rerank", ) nodes = await hybrid_retriever.aretrieve("machine learning applications")

rule.statement: Use RouterQueryEngine with LLMSingleSelector when queries map cleanly to one of several distinct indexes; use SubQuestionQueryEngine to decompose complex multi-part questions that require answers from multiple indexes in parallel.
rule.rationale: RouterQueryEngine is cheaper (one routing call); SubQuestionQueryEngine parallelizes sub-queries with use_async=True for throughput. Use clear, specific tool descriptions — the LLM router makes selection based solely on the description text.

example.code:
from llama_index.core.query_engine import SubQuestionQueryEngine from llama_index.core.tools import QueryEngineTool, ToolMetadata
tools = [ QueryEngineTool( query_engine=tech_index.as_query_engine(), metadata=ToolMetadata( name="technical_docs", description="Technical documentation, API references, and architecture specs", ), ), QueryEngineTool( query_engine=business_index.as_query_engine(), metadata=ToolMetadata( name="business_docs", description="Business reports, financial data, and strategy documents", ), ), ] query_engine = SubQuestionQueryEngine.from_defaults( query_engine_tools=tools, use_async=True, verbose=True, )
example.note: SubQuestionQueryEngine runs sub-queries sequentially by default. Always pass use_async=True for parallelism.

antipattern.description: Vague tool descriptions in RouterQueryEngine ("handles documents about everything").
antipattern.reason: The LLM router selects based on description match to the query. Vague descriptions cause misrouting. Each description must be specific enough that the router can distinguish which index to use for a given query type.
